steps.title: Translate the question
steps.content: The predicted $y$-value is the height of the line of best fit at the same $x$-value as the data point. The actual $y$-value is the height of the dot. So a point counts exactly when the dot is above the line.

steps.title: Count by residual sign
steps.content: The line of best fit is approximately $y=x$. Moving left to right, the dots above the line are near $x\approx0.3$, $1.0$, $2.0$, $4.2$, $6.1$, and $9.2$. The dots near $x\approx2.8$, $3.7$, $4.8$, and $7.4$ are below the line.

steps.title: Match the count
steps.content: There are $6$ points above the line, so there are $6$ data points whose actual $y$-value is greater than the predicted $y$-value. That matches choice C.
